title: Do trading eas actually work and make you profitable?
author: EndlessKnight_154
selftext:
I work as a broker, and I constantly see people promoting “profitable” EAs, AI trading bots, and fully automated strategies. Every week there’s a new one claiming crazy returns, sometimes 10,000%+, which already feels unrealistic.

From experience, EAs *can* work, but the real ones are usually simple, boring, and very strict with risk. They don’t run forever and they definitely aren’t set-and-forget money printers. Execution and uptime matter too, which is why serious users run them on a VPS.

Curious to hear honest experiences. Have you actually seen an EA or trading bot stay profitable long term in live trading, or is most of this industry just marketing and overfitted backtests?

comments.author: Adventurous_Chart360
comments.body: I am not claiming 1000%>> but try tradingbotengine.con and see the historical predictions on the site.

comments.author: Creative-System-2768
comments.body: I tried, but it's hard to make money quick on EA. If you use many years of data and get a profitable strategy then it's not shared there, you got to buy it outside from meta5.

comments.author: Puzzleheaded_Ad_4478
comments.body:
Yes there are some bots that are profitable on the long run. Some are clearly scams, no need to remind this when you see 10000%+ unrealistic returns. 

You'll be surprised how simple the most profitable strategies are.

comments.author: Patient-Bumblebee
comments.body: Just vibetrade. Its free. Alpha is in the prompt. Use a vibetrading exchange with zero fees.
